mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which correlates with higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening to combat inflation, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 305, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of observed periods, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 60-day equity returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of contraction periods, or the signal fails to distinguish between temporary dips and sustained downturns.
